title: TypeScript is not TypeScript anymore...
summary: TypeScript 7 is a complete recompile of the TypeScript compiler from TypeScript into Go, achieving approximately 10x performance improvement. The release includes shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and a rewritten watch mode while maintaining backward compatibility with existing type checking output.
writeup: This video covers the TypeScript 7 release candidate, which rewrites the compiler from TypeScript into Go and delivers roughly a 10x performance improvement. The rewrite preserves output compatibility while adding shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and an optimized watch mode. The shift matters because it changes the language's implementation foundation and targets the compile and type checking bottlenecks that affect large projects.
